source_paper_title: Rates of Convergence in the Central Limit Theorem for Markov Chains, with an Application to TD Learning
context:
Section 5 (Conclusions), bullet beginning “Under some conditions, the rate of convergence in the central limit theorem for i.i.d. random vectors is O(1/\sqrt n) …” (page 19 of 24).

“Under some conditions, the rate of convergence in the central limit theorem for i.i.d. random vectors is O(1/\sqrt n) (Fang et al. 2019). It would be interesting to see if the O(log n/\sqrt n) rate of convergence in Theorem 1 can be improved to O(1/\sqrt n) under possibly additional conditions than the ones used in this paper.”
title: Eliminate the logarithmic factor in the non-asymptotic martingale/Markov-chain CLT rate in Wasserstein distance
latex:
Given a filtered probability space \((\Omega,\mathcal F,\{\mathcal F_k\}_{k\ge 0},\mathbb P)\) and a \(d\)-dimensional martingale difference sequence \(\{m_k\}_{k\ge 1}\) with \(\mathbb E[m_k\mid \mathcal F_{k-1}]=0\), define \(S_n:=\sum_{k=1}^n m_k\) and \(W_n:=S_n/\sqrt n\). Assume there exists a positive definite matrix \(\Sigma_\infty\succ 0\) such that \(\Sigma_k:=\mathbb E[m_k m_k^\top\mid \mathcal F_{k-1}]\) satisfies \(\mathbb E\,\Sigma_k\to \Sigma_\infty\) and suitable moment and mixing/ergodicity conditions ensuring a martingale central limit theorem and, via Poisson’s equation, a Markov-chain CLT for \(U_n:=\frac{1}{\sqrt n}\sum_{k=1}^n(r(X_{k-1})-\bar r)\) with asymptotic covariance \(\Sigma_\infty\).

Let \(d_W\) denote the \(1\)-Wasserstein distance on \(\mathbb R^d\):
\[
 d_W(X,Y):=\sup_{h\in\mathrm{Lip}_1}\,\mathbb E\,[h(X)-h(Y)],
\]
where \(\mathrm{Lip}_1\) is the set of 1-Lipschitz functions.

Problem: Find verifiable conditions (e.g., strengthened conditional moment, conditional variance stabilization, or Markov-chain regularity assumptions) under which the non-asymptotic Wasserstein CLT rate can be improved from \(O(\log n/\sqrt n)\) to the optimal Berry–Esseen-type rate \(O(1/\sqrt n)\), i.e., establish
\[
 d_W\bigl(W_n,\Sigma_\infty^{1/2}Z\bigr)\le \frac{C}{\sqrt n}
\quad\text{and/or}\quad
 d_W\bigl(U_n,\Sigma_\infty^{1/2}Z\bigr)\le \frac{C}{\sqrt n},
\]
for all \(n\ge 1\) with a constant \(C\) independent of \(n\), where \(Z\sim\mathcal N(0,I_d)\).

It would be interesting to see if the O(log n/\sqrt n) rate of convergence in Theorem 1 can be improved to O(1/\sqrt n) under possibly additional conditions than the ones used in this paper.”
